mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.16% indicates deep accommodation
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 9% signals labor market slack and recessionary pressure that depresses corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking investors reprice risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 9%, or correlation between UNRATE and 5-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts remain below 600k units, indicating weakening consumer demand and residential investment that compress aggregate demand and equity earnings growth over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth < 2% YoY AND HOUST < 600k units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while remaining below 96.5 signals supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization that constrains profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with declining industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of instances when INDPRO < 96.5 and declining, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 50k per month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating labor market deterioration that signals recessionary dynamics and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50k AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131488k and UNRATE at 9.4% indicate labor stress
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with simultaneous employment and unemployment pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth remains below 2% annualized while inflation-adjusted retail sales decline, indicating demand-side stagnation that compresses equity earnings growth and forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS real growth < 1% YoY
regime_hint: low-growth demand regime with stagnation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 3% while federal funds rate remains below 1%, indicating negative real rates that erode fixed-income returns and redirect capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% well below 1%, CPIAUCSL at 214.47
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction term has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above 97.5 while payroll employment grows above 100k per month, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 below threshold, PAYEMS at 131488k
regime_hint: synchronized expansion with supply and labor growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year windows.
